other_observers: It was seen well by most participants of the DFO trip that day, including Andrew Spencer, Joey Kellner, Norm Erthal, Lisa Edwards, Allison Hilf, Steve Larson, Paul Differding, and others. The only person on the trip who missed good looks was Joe Roll; Andrew Spencer found the bird.
specific_location: In deciduous trees right along the inlet canal at Prewitt Reservoir SWA, maybe 100 yards west of the end of the trees.
description: A slender warbler, kinda longish-tailed; uniform slate-gray above: no wingbars or streaks. Underparts yellow. Yellow throat set off from below by faint necklace of dark streaks, stronger towards sides, where I think they extended a short distance down the sides. Distinct circular white eye ring. Bill, as seen from below in foreshortened view, had a distinct flesh color.Behaviors: The bird was very active, foraging from 10-20 feet up in thick deciduous woods in the company of a great many other small passerines. It liked the thicker vegetation and switched trees often.Call: The single chip note I THINK came from this bird was thick and noisy, rather like that of a Wilson's Warbler.Plumage: believed to be adult female
